steps.title: Write the purchase equations
steps.content:
Let $b$ be the cost of one loaf of bread and $e$ be the cost of one dozen eggs. The purchases give two equations.
$$b+2e=12.89$$
$$4b+e=19.92$$

steps.title: Eliminate the bread cost
steps.content:
Multiply the first equation by $4$, then subtract the second equation.
$$4b+8e=51.56$$
$$(4b+8e)-(4b+e)=51.56-19.92$$
$$7e=31.64$$

steps.title: Solve for eggs
steps.content:
Divide by $7$ to find the cost of one dozen eggs.
$$e=\frac{31.64}{7}=4.52$$
The answer is D.
